title: Reconstitution of circularized nanodiscs using SpyCatcher-SpyTag
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"9ck92","text":"Circularized nanodiscs (cNDs) with diameters over 20 nm are useful tools for biochemical reconstitutions of membrane protein complexes1. However, the application of these large cNDs is limited by low yields and time-consuming procedures. To address this challenge, we herein present a protocol to ease the reconstitution of large cNDs using the SpyCatcher-SpyTag technology.  ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"sup","offset":134,"length":1}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"3leop","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}

steps: Thaw 1 vial of BL21 STAR TM (DE3) cells on ice.
Add 1 µL of spMSP plasmids (10-50 ng/ µL) into the vial and keep it on ice for 10 minutes.
Heat shock at 42 °C for 30 seconds and place the vial on ice for 2 minutes.
Add 500 µL LB broth into the vial and shake it at 37 °C for 1 hour.
Spread the cells on LB agar Kanamycin plates.
Incubate the plates overnight at 37 °C.
A single colony from the plate was picked into 10 ml LB media supplemented with 50 µg/ml kanamycin. Shake the culture at 37 °C overnight (200 rpm).
Transfer the 10 ml culture into 1 L LB supplemented with 50 µg/ml kanamycin. Incubate the 1L culture at 37 °C with shaking at 200 rpm until OD 600 = 0.7.
Add IPTG (0.2 mM) into the culture and continue incubation with shaking at 120 rpm for 20h at 16 °C.
Collect the cells by centrifugation at 3450 x g for 20 minutes using 5920 R.
Cells were resuspended in buffer A (50 mM Tris-HCl (pH 8),100 mM NaCl, 5% glycerol, 2 mM β-mercaptoethanol) plus one cOmplete tablet.
Cells were lysed on ice using a Branson cell disrupter (60% duty cycle, 45 secs).
Cell lysate was clarified by centrifugation at 12, 000 x g for 45 mins using JXN26.

The supernatant was loaded onto a 1 ml Ni 2+ -NTA column, followed by extensive wash (20 column volume) using buffer B (50 mM Tris-HCl (pH 8), 20 mM Imidazole, 400 mM NaCl, 5% glycerol, 2 mM β-mercaptoethanol). Proteins were eluted in buffer C (50 mM Tris-HCl (pH 8), 500 mM Imidazole, 400 mM NaCl, 5% glycerol, 2 mM β-mercaptoethanol), desalted in buffer A using PD MiDiTrap G-25 (GE Healthcare), and stored at -80 °C.

Purified spMSPs were incubated with PC lipids at different ratios in buffer A containing 0.05% DDM. Samples were kept on ice for 30 min, and detergents were slowly removed with BioBeads (1/3 volume) and gentle shaking (4 °C, overnight).

cNDs were fractionated by size exclusion chromatography (SEC) using the Superose 6 10/300 in buffer A. Fractions corresponding to cNDs were kept and subjected to analysis by negative stain electron microscopy.
Formvar/carbon-coated copper grids were glow discharged (15 mA, 25 secs) using PELCO easiGlow TM .
cNDs (10 µg/ml) were applied onto the grids for 30 secs, followed by staining with 0.75% uranyl formate for 1 minute.
Images were collected using a ThermoFisher Science Tecnai G2 TEM (100 kV) equipped with a Veleta CCD camera (Olympus).
If polydisperse particles are formed from SEC, protein: lipid ratios need to be optimized.

Day-1, Preparation of LB plates and media. Day 0, Transformation of spMSP plasmids into BL21 cells. Day 1-2, Protein expression in BL21 cells. Day 3, spMSP purification and cND reconstitution. Day 4, Fractionation of cNDs by SEC. Day 5, Characterization of purified cNDs by negative stain electron microscopy.
The described protocol is able to generate highly monodisperse cNDs. cNDs should show the expected size by negative stain EM.

One-step construction of circularized nanodiscs using SpyCatcher-SpyTag by Shanwen Zhang, Qian Ren, Scott J. Novick, Timothy S. Strutzenberg, Patrick R. Griffin, Huan Bao published in Nature Communications on 14 Sep, 2021 https://dx.doi.org/10.1038/s41467-021-25737-7
creator: Protocols Importer
